overview: Processes **HTML5** documents, including those containing **ITS 2.0** (Internationalization Tag Set) markup. Input documents are expected to be valid HTML5. The filter supports global and local ITS rules and most ITS 2.0 data categories, enabling fine-grained control over translatable content, terminology, language information, and directionality.

path.help:
Path to an **ITS rules file** that overrides the default HTML5 processing behavior.

The default behavior includes:
- `lang` attribute → Language Information
- `id` attribute → Id Value
- Phrasing content elements treated as inline (`withinText="yes"`)
- `translate` attribute → Translate data category (using HTML5-specific behavior, not XML)

Provide a custom ITS document to override any of these defaults. See the [ITS specification](http://www.w3.org/TR/its20/) for the rule format.
path.values: Path to an ITS 2.0 XML rules document (`.its` or `.xml`)

simplifierRules.help: Rules that control how inline codes are simplified for translation. These rules map HTML inline elements to shorter placeholder representations, making segments easier to translate.

moveLeadingAndTrailingCodesToSkeleton.help: Inline codes (e.g., `<b>`, `<span>`) that appear at the very start or end of a segment are moved out of the translatable content and into the skeleton. This simplifies segments for translators when boundary tags don't affect the translatable text.

mergeAdjacentCodes.help: When multiple inline codes appear next to each other with no text between them (e.g., ``), they are combined into a single placeholder code. This reduces visual clutter in segments presented to translators.

processingNotes: **Input encoding** is auto-detected using the HTML5 specification's encoding detection mechanism.
**Output encoding** matches the input encoding unless overridden by the calling tool.
**Line-breaks** in the output match the original input line-break style.

**Quote escaping** can be configured via `quoteModeDefined` and `quoteMode` parameters in the config file. Modes: `0` = unescaped, `1` = all named entities, `2` = named for double / numeric for single, `3` = double quotes only.

The filter uses HTML5-specific `translate` attribute behavior, which differs from the XML ITS behavior. See the [HTML5 spec](http://www.w3.org/TR/2013/CR-html5-20130806/dom.html#the-translate-attribute) for details.
ITS rules in the input document itself will override the default behavior, as will rules from a custom parameters file.

examples.title: Default ITS rules for HTML5
examples.description: The default configuration includes ITS rules that define standard HTML5 translatable attributes, non-translatable elements (`<script>`, `<style>`, `<del>`), bidirectional markup, and preserve-space elements (`<pre>`, `<textarea>`). Custom ITS rules files can override any of these defaults.
examples.config:
```xml
<its:rules xmlns:its="http://www.w3.org/2005/11/its" version="2.0"
 xmlns:h="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml">
 <!-- Non-translatable elements -->
 <its:translateRule selector="//h:script|//h:style" translate="no"/>
 <its:translateRule selector="//h:del" translate="no"/>

 <!-- Translatable attributes -->
 <its:translateRule selector="//h:*/@alt|//h:*/@title" translate="yes"/>
 <its:translateRule selector="//h:meta[@name='keywords']/@content" translate="yes"/>
 <its:translateRule selector="//h:meta[@name='description']/@content" translate="yes"/>

 <!-- Preserve space -->
 <its:preserveSpaceRule selector="//h:pre|//h:textarea" space="preserve"/>
</its:rules>
```

examples.title: Quote mode configuration
examples.description: Configure how quotes and apostrophes are escaped in the output by setting `quoteModeDefined` and `quoteMode` in the config file.

path.title: ITS Parameters File
path.description: Path to an ITS 2.0 rules document that overrides the default HTML5 processing behavior

fullDoc: # HTML5-ITS Filter Processes **HTML5** documents, including those containing **ITS 2.0** (Internationalization Tag Set) markup. Input documents are expected to be valid HTML5. The filter supports global and local ITS rules and most ITS 2.0 data categories, enabling fine-grained control over translatable content, terminology, language information, and directionality. ## Parameters ### ITS Parameters File (`path`) Path to an **ITS rules file** that overrides the default HTML5 processing behavior. The default behavior includes: - `lang` attribute → Language Information - `id` attribute → Id Value - Phrasing content elements treated as inline (`withinText="yes"`) - `translate` attribute → Translate data category (using HTML5-specific behavior, not XML) Provide a custom ITS document to override any of these defaults. ### Simplifier Rules (`simplifierRules`) Rules that control how inline codes are simplified for translation. These rules map HTML inline elements to shorter placeholder representations, making segments easier to translate. ### Move Boundary Codes to Skeleton (`moveLeadingAndTrailingCodesToSkeleton`) Inline codes (e.g., ``, ``) that appear at the very start or end of a segment are moved out of the translatable content and into the skeleton. This simplifies segments for translators when boundary tags don't affect the translatable text. ### Merge Adjacent Codes (`mergeAdjacentCodes`) When multiple inline codes appear next to each other with no text between them (e.g., ``), they are combined into a single placeholder code. This reduces visual clutter in segments presented to translators. ## Quote Mode Escaping of quote and apostrophe characters can be configured via config file properties: | Mode | Value | Behavior | |------|-------|----------| | `0` | UNESCAPED | Do not escape single or double quotes | | `1` | ALL | Escape both to named entities | | `2` | NUMERIC_SINGLE_QUOTES | Named entity for double quotes, numeric for single | | `3` | DOUBLE_QUOTES_ONLY | Escape double quotes only | ## ITS Support The filter processes HTML5 documents based on ITS defaults: - The `lang` attribute maps to the **Language Information** data category - The `id` attribute maps to the **Id Value** data category - Phrasing content elements are treated as inline (`withinText="yes"`) - The `translate` attribute maps to the **Translate** data category (HTML5-specific behavior) Default behavior can be overridden by ITS markup in the input document or by specifying a custom ITS parameters file. The filter supports ITS 2.0 — see the [W3C ITS 2.0 specification](http://www.w3.org/TR/its20/) for details. ## Limitations - This filter is **BETA**. ## Notes - Input encoding is auto-detected per the HTML5 specification - Output encoding matches input unless overridden by the calling tool - Line-breaks in output match the original input style - HTML5 `translate` attribute behavior differs from XML ITS — see the [HTML5 spec](http://www.w3.org/TR/2013/CR-html5-20130806/dom.html#the-translate-attribute) ## Examples ### Default ITS Rules The default configuration defines standard HTML5 behavior: ```xml ``` ### Quote Mode Configuration ```properties quoteModeDefined=true quoteMode=3 ```
